name: MISP (Malware Information Sharing Platform)
pricing_notes: Open source (AGPL-3.0); CIRCL (Luxembourg CERT) / MISP Project

auth.notes: MISP API key (per user); Authorization header

performance.notes: Self-hosted platform; search performance depends on event count

agent_readiness.notes: Threat intelligence platform for sharing IoCs. PyMISP client. misp.add_event(), misp.search(). Events contain attributes (IP, domain, hash, URL) and objects. Taxonomies, galaxies (threat actors, malware families, techniques). STIX 2.0 export. Sharing groups for controlled distribution. Free feeds (CIRCL, Feodo Tracker, etc.). Correlates IoCs across events automatically. Standard in national CERTs, ISACs, SOCs. TheHive integration for case management.

gotchas: Complex data model (events/attributes/objects/galaxies) has learning curve
AGPL license requires source disclosure for modifications
UI and API can feel dated
Requires significant infrastructure for large-scale deployment
best_when: CTI teams building threat intelligence sharing infrastructure
CERTs and ISACs sharing IoCs with partner organizations
SOCs wanting free threat intel with IoC correlation
Integrating threat intel into TheHive incident response workflow
avoid_when: Commercial CTI needs (ThreatConnect, Anomali more user-friendly)
Simple IoC enrichment (use VirusTotal or AlienVault OTX API)
Small teams without dedicated CTI analyst
summary: MISP is the standard open-source threat intelligence platform used by CERTs, ISACs, and SOCs. Events with IoC attributes (IPs, domains, hashes). Taxonomies, threat actor galaxies, MITRE ATT&CK mapping. Auto-correlation across events. STIX 2.0 export. Sharing groups. PyMISP Python client. TheHive integration. Free global feeds. Complex data model but essential for serious CTI operations.
